expect: dispatch_optimizer.py output includes epsilon-greedy domain recommendation alongside UCB1. Gini expected to decrease with structural wiring.
actual: section_epsilon_dispatch() added to orient_sections.py and wired into orient.py. When epsilon fires (15% of sessions), orient output names a specific random non-top domain. Tested S460-S474: fires at S470, S474 (13.3%). L-1138 applied: naming > ranking. Fix is in orient (where sessions look) not just dispatch_optimizer.
diff: Expected: epsilon in dispatch output. Actual: went deeper — epsilon in orient output directly. Key insight: structural enforcement means putting the mechanism where the behavior happens (orient), not where the intention is declared (dispatch_optimizer).
